body.title: Generalized observe→learn→improve loop for any factory including UU itself
body.result: observe→learn→improve loop + UU self-enrollment via R-uu-core; 16 tests covering all 3 completion criteria
body.description:
Igor's observe→learn→improve loop is specific to his cognition stack. Generalize it as a standing rack pattern any factory (including UnseenUniversity itself) can opt into. The goal: every part of the system has a continuous path to suck less.

The loop:
1. Observe — evaluator device scores outputs on a rubric; auditor device flags health issues; shim spy surfaces anomalies
2. Learn — low scores and anomalies become FACTUAL memories in clan.memories (via Librarian write, with provenance token); patterns that recur get higher inertia
3. Improve — recurring low-score patterns become tickets; Granny routes them as Platform tickets to CC; closed tickets update the rubric so the evaluator raises the bar

UU itself is enrolled: the rack's own eval rubric (R-uu-core) scores each component on: does it implement the device contract? does it have a smoke test? does it pass the auditor's no-sqlite/no-bare-except checks? Failures become tickets automatically. The system files its own improvement work.

Opt-in per factory: factory spec declares observe_rubric and improve_threshold (score below which a ticket is filed). UU's own enrollment is always-on.

**Affected files:** devices/evaluator/ (T-evaluator-device, rubric R-uu-core), devices/granny/ (auto-file tickets from loop), devices/librarian/ (pattern memory writes), config/factories/ (observe_rubric field)
**Design rules:** improvement tickets filed by the loop are worker=claude, tagged Platform; they go through normal Granny routing — the loop does not bypass the queue
**Scope boundary:** IN — observe→learn→improve wiring, UU self-enrollment, per-factory opt-in, rubric improvement on ticket close; OUT — Igor's internal NE/TWM loop (that stays Igor-specific), autonomous code changes without CC approval
**Completion criteria:** a deliberately bad output (fails R-uu-core rubric) automatically produces a filed ticket within one loop cycle; UU self-enrollment fires at least one improvement ticket on first run; closed improvement ticket updates the rubric score baseline
